validation_rules: If totals exist: total ~= subtotal + tax
If line items exist: total ~= sum(line_items.amount)
instructions:
Extraction Template Instructions for Flood Brothers Invoice PDFs

General:
- Remove duplicate/summary pages and process only the first occurrence of all header fields.
- Normalize all dates to YYYY-MM-DD.
- Remove commas, currency symbols, and whitespace padding from number fields.
- Remove extra linebreaks within address fields; keep single linebreaks between address parts.

Field Extraction:

File Name:
- Set to the input PDF file name.

hauler_name:
- Extract text near top of the first page matching "FLOOD BROTHERS DISPOSAL" (with or without "& RECYCLING SERVICES").
- Take the first occurrence.

billing_date:
- Anchor to label: "BILLING DATE:", "BILL DATE:", "DATE:", "Billing Date:"
- Extract date string immediately after the label.
- Normalize.

invoice_number:
- Anchor to label: "INVOICE NO:", "Invoice No", "Invoice #"
- Extract the number after the label.

customer_number:
- Anchor to label: "CUSTOMER NO:", "Account No", "Account Number", "Customer Number"
- Extract immediately following value.

bill_to:
- Anchor to "BILL TO:" (address block follows on subsequent lines).
- Capture all contiguous lines until next known field or blank line.
- Normalize as multi-line string.

service_address:
- Anchor to "SERVICE ADDRESS:" (address block follows on subsequent lines).
- Capture all contiguous lines until next known field or blank line.
- Normalize as multi-line string.

total_due_balance:
- Anchor to "TOTAL BALANCE DUE", "BALANCE DUE", or "TOTAL DUE"
- If balance printed over multiple lines, use the numeric value directly underneath/beside those labels.
- Remove any non-numeric characters (commas, currency).
- Use the first matching occurrence.

due_date:
- Anchor to "DATE DUE:", "Due Date", "DUE DATE"
- Extract date string immediately after label.
- Normalize.

line_service_address:
- In line item sections, look for "SUB ACCT" lines and capture next address line matching "{number} VINCENNES RD" as service address for following line items.
- For main header, extract address line under SERVICE ADDRESS that matches pattern "{number} VINCENNES RD" (this is the line_service_address).

Line Item Extraction:

Locate the table region(s) by presence of these recurring headers (or in repeated blocks):
- Look for rows with fields: "DATE", "DESCRIPTION", "QUANTITY", "RATE", "TOTAL".

For each line item:
- If within a "SUB ACCT" or block with a recognized address, assign that service address until the next address is found.
- "line_date": Extract date at start of an item block; if only in MM/DD/YY, convert to YYYY-MM-DD.
- "line_description": Extract item description, typically first text line after date, stopping before numeric columns.
- "quantity": Extract first number after description.
- "rate": Extract if present (if missing, set as empty).
- "line_total": Extract numeric value after quantity/rate columns (ignore lines labeled "Sub Total", "Total", use only for actual item lines).
- Assign "File Name" to each line item.

Normalization:
- Dates: Convert all to "YYYY-MM-DD".
- Numbers: Remove commas and currency symbols; parse as number.
- For blank or missing fields, set as empty string per schema.

Notes:
- Only extract the first logical page's header fields (ignore repeats caused by copy pages).
- Assign only the first "line_service_address" that appears under SERVICE ADDRESS in the header block for the document (not per line).
- For line items, if repeated across multiple sub-accounts, treat each sub-account/address block as a new item/group.
- Exclude summary/total/account aging lines; include only actual service line items.
